messages: I also sometimes have an espresso in the late afternoon to get through long workdays, which I suspect doesn't help my nights.
On top of the external noise, my partner snores and we share the same small bedroom, so separating isn't really realistic right now.
What are some practical ways to address noise coming from both outside and the bed partner without creating friction?
To be clear, I'm looking for realistic suggestions that don't involve moving, major renovations, or a big budget.
Is cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia adaptable to a schedule and living situation with limited flexibility?
If you had to choose three priorities for the first two weeks given limits on time, space, and money, what would they be?
Can you outline a concise, roughly 30-60 minute pre-bed routine that helps with falling asleep and with falling back asleep after brief awakenings?
What's the most cost-effective way to manage ambient city noise in a small bedroom without compromising alarms or safety?
For the sleep-stiffness, could you suggest specific pillow arrangements or inexpensive supports that reduce morning lumbar pain?
How can I adjust late-afternoon caffeine or nap habits so I stay alert during the day without wrecking the night?
Are there particular tracker features or ways of looking at data that tend to be genuinely helpful rather than anxiety-inducing?
What are low-drama, low-effort ways to bring up and negotiate sleep changes with a partner so both people get better rest?
If behavioral changes don't lead to clear improvements after a few weeks, when does it make sense to pursue a clinical evaluation or sleep study?
Could you give me a one-week trial checklist with concrete nightly steps and simple criteria for whether to continue or modify the approach?
Also, what quick morning strategies reduce that groggy 'can't think straight' feeling on days I have to be up very early?

Given everything I've shared across these messages, please design a practical four-week plan—week-by-week with daily routines, low-cost bedroom tweaks, middle-of-night strategies, simple lumbar-support fixes, noise-management approaches for both external and partner sounds, caffeine and nap guidance that avoids routine nightly meds, and measurable milestones to track progress—tailored to someone with tight early mornings and a small urban living space.
